title: Vector CANoe Hardware Debugger Binding Configuration Schema

full.title: Debugger Configuration Version 1.1
full.description: Debugger configuration for the Vector CANoe hardware debugger binding (version 1.1)

version.title: Version
version.description: The version of the configuration. Controls what features are available

default-debugger.title: Default Debugger
default-debugger.description: The default debugger if a distributed object is not assigned explicitly. Must reference an element from the `debuggers` list

debuggers.title: Debugger Configurations
debuggers.description: List of all configured debuggers. The elements' key determines the name of the debugger

reset.title: Target Reset
reset.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will cause a target reset. Must be supported by the target

run.title: Start Execution
run.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will start execution of the program. Must be supported by the target

auto-connect.title: Auto Connect
auto-connect.description: Automatically connects to the TRACE32 instance on measurement start.

node.title: Node Address
node.description: The IP address of the node where the TRACE32 instance is running

port.title: Node Port
port.description: The port on which the TRACE32 instance is listening.

pack-len.title: TRACE32 Package Length
pack-len.description: The UDP package length. Must match the package length configured for the TRACE32 instance

reset.title: Target Reset
reset.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will cause a target reset. Must be supported by the target

run.title: Start Execution
run.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will start execution of the program. Must be supported by the target

auto-connect.title: Auto Connect
auto-connect.description: Automatically connects to the winIDEA instance on measurement start.

node.title: Node Address
node.description: The IP address of the node where the winIDEA instance is running

port.title: Node Port
port.description: The port on which the winIDEA instance is listening.

isystem.title: iSYSTEM / TASKING winIDEA Settings
isystem.description: Debugger specific settings for winIDEA (iSYSTEM / TASKING)

disable-winidea-polling.title: Disable winIDEA GUI Polling
disable-winidea-polling.description: Disables the polling of the winIDEA GUI. When set to `true`, it can reduce jitter when communicating with the SIL Adapter on the target. Makes interactive work with the GUI not possible

reset.title: Target Reset
reset.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will cause a target reset. Must be supported by the target

run.title: Start Execution
run.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will start execution of the program. Must always be true for GDB

auto-connect.title: Auto Connect
auto-connect.description: Automatically connects to the GDB server on measurement start.

node.title: Node Address
node.description: The IP address of the node where the GDB server is running

port.title: Node Port
port.description: The port on which the GDB server is listening.

path-to-gdb-client.title: GDB Client
path-to-gdb-client.description: Absolute or relative path to the GDB client, which shall be used by CANoe. Path is relative to this configuration file. This is usually supplied with the compiler toolset

path-to-symbol-file.title: GDB Symbol File
path-to-symbol-file.description: Absolute or relative path to the symbol file, which shall be used by GDB. Path is relative to this configuration file. Symbol file must match to the binary running on the target

oneOf.$comment: DO NOT ADD NEW FIELDS TO, OR EXTEND EXISTING FIELDS OF THIS ENTRY. This entry is deprecated.
oneOf.title: DEPRECATED Debugger Configuration (iSYSTEM)
oneOf.description: DEPRECATED Debugger Configuration for winIDEA (iSYSTEM). Replaced by the 'Debugger Configuration (TASKING winIDEA)''.

node.title: Node Address
node.description: The IP address of the node where the GDB server is running

port.title: Node Port
port.description: The port on which the GDB server is listening.

path-to-gdb-client.title: GDB Client
path-to-gdb-client.description: Absolute or relative path to the GDB client, which shall be used by CANoe. Path is relative to this configuration file. This is usually supplied with the compiler toolset

path-to-symbol-file.title: GDB Symbol File
path-to-symbol-file.description: Absolute or relative path to the symbol file, which shall be used by GDB. Path is relative to this configuration file. Symbol file must match to the binary running on the target

oneOf.title: Debugger Configuration (SEGGER J-Link over TCP/IP)
oneOf.description: Debugger Configuration for SEGGER J-Link over TCP/IP

node.title: Debugger Address
node.description: The hostname or IP address of the SEGGER J-Link debugger.

port.title: Debugger Port
port.description: The port on which the SEGGER J-Link is listening. Using value 0 will use the default port.

reset.title: Target Reset
reset.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will cause a target reset. Must be supported by the target

run.title: Start Execution
run.description: Connecting to the target on measurement start will start execution of the program. Must be supported by the target

auto-connect.title: Auto Connect
auto-connect.description: Automatically connects to the SEGGER J-Link instance on measurement start.

segger-jlink.title: SEGGER J-Link Settings
segger-jlink.description: Debugger specific settings for SEGGER J-Link

path-to-symbol-file.title: Symbol File
path-to-symbol-file.description: Absolute or relative path to the symbol file. Path is relative to this configuration file. Symbol file must match to the binary running on the target.

device.title: Device
device.description: The device name of the target as specified by the list of supported devices on the SEGGER website. The device will be selected by executing the `device = ...` Command String. See [Supported Devices](https://www.segger.com/supported-devices) for more.

target-interface.title: Target Interface
target-interface.description: The target interface the SEGGER J-Link will use to connect to the device.

oneOf.title: SWD Target Interface
oneOf.description: Instructs the debugger to use the Serial Wire Debug (SWD) interface to connect to the device.

oneOf.title: JTAG Target Interface
oneOf.description: Instructs the debugger to use the Joint Test Action Group (JTAG) interface to connect to the device.

speed.title: Target Interface Speed
speed.description: The speed at which the SEGGER J-Link will connect to the device over the target interface in kHz. The value 0 will instruct the SEGGER J-Link to autodetect the speed. The value 65535 will instruct the SEGGER J-Link to use adaptive clocking.

jlink-dll-path.title: Path to the JLinkARM.dll or JLink_x64.dll
jlink-dll-path.description: Absolute or relative path to the DLL file. Path is relative to this configuration file. If not present, an attempt to automatically find an installed JLink DLL is made. The bitness (32 / 64) of the selected DLL must match the runtime kernel bitness, otherwise loading the DLL will fail.

jlink-commands.title: Additional SEGGER J-Link Command Strings
jlink-commands.description: List of additional SEGGER J-Link command strings that are executed directly after the device is selected, but before the target interface and speed are selected. See [J-Link Command Strings](https://kb.segger.com/J-Link_Command_Strings) for the full list of supported command strings.

oneOf.$comment: IMPORTANT: Keep all fields except 'serial-number' / 'node' + 'port' in sync. with the TCP/IP variant.
oneOf.title: Debugger Configuration (SEGGER J-Link over USB)
oneOf.description: Debugger Configuration for SEGGER J-Link over USB

reset.title: Target Reset

segger-jlink.title: SEGGER J-Link Settings
segger-jlink.description: Debugger specific settings for SEGGER J-Link

oneOf.title: Serial Number of the Debugger
oneOf.description: The serial number of the SEGGER J-Link debugger.

allOf.title: Serial Number of the Debugger
allOf.description: The serial number of the SEGGER J-Link debugger.

oneOf.title: Serial Number of the Debugger
oneOf.description: Using the value 'auto' will automatically select a debugger connected via USB. Automatic selection will fail if more than one debugger is connected.

path-to-symbol-file.title: Symbol File
path-to-symbol-file.description: Absolute or relative path to the symbol file. Path is relative to this configuration file. Symbol file must match to the binary running on the target.

device.title: Device

target-interface.title: Target Interface

The bitness (32 / 64) of the selected DLL must match the runtime kernel bitness, otherwise loading the DLL will fail.

jlink-commands.description: List of additional SEGGER J-Link command strings that are executed directly after the device is selected, but before the target interface and speed are selected. See [J-Link Command Strings](https://kb.segger.com/J-Link_Command_Strings) for the full list of supported command strings.
